title: Delicate Andalusian Eggs Custard
description: A silky, herbal custard made with free‑range eggs and kombu extract, set with agar‑agar for a glassy texture, perfect for a lazy Sunday treat.
instructions: Preheat oven to 160F and fill a larger baking pan with water to create a bain‑mare.
In a saucepan, combine the whole milk and 50 ml of kombu cold extract and heat gently until it reaches 70C.
Whisk the free‑range eggs, caster sugar, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt in a bowl until the mixture is pale and slightly thickened.
Gradually pour the 70C milk mixture into the eggs while whisking constantly to prevent curdling.
Sprinkle 1 g of agar‑agar powder over the custard and whisk until it is fully dissolved.
Grease individual ramekins with 1 tsp of unsalted butter and pour the custard mixture into them, leaving a small gap at the top.
Place the ramekins in the prepared bain‑mare and bake for 25 minutes, or until the custard edges set but the center remains slightly wobbly.
Remove from the oven, let cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours before serving, garnished with lemon zest or a dusting of powdered cinnamon.
